text: Artificial intelligence is becoming more common in our daily lives, but it also raises important ethical questions. One major concern is bias: if AI systems are trained on data that contains human biases, they may produce unfair outcomes, for example in hiring or loan applications. Fairness means ensuring that AI treats all people equally, regardless of background or identity. Privacy is another key issue, as AI often collects and analyzes personal information, which could be misused. To address these challenges, responsible AI development requires transparency, meaning that companies should clearly explain how their algorithms work and make decisions. By focusing on these principles, we can create AI that benefits everyone while minimizing harm.
